title: ferray-core: no buffer protocol / FFI-safe array descriptor
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy implements Python's buffer protocol (PEP 3118), allowing zero-copy sharing of array memory with PIL, pandas, PyTorch, etc. The buffer protocol exports shape, strides, format string, and a pointer. ferray has AsRawBuffer for raw pointer access but no standardized descriptor that external code can use to interpret the buffer. For C FFI and PyO3 interop, a stable ABI struct (pointer, shape, strides, dtype tag, ndim) would enable zero-copy exchange with C libraries and Python objects without going through Arrow.
